textByHash.1a546fd6c20965e6b00cf2a60f8bf25caa57df71: Oh yes. In fact, I sell to humans far more than I sell to tortugans. Most of our kind have no issues with catching their own fish, and can normally do so without all these tools you humans use.
textByHash.54a3cb1ed2033c55a469869726d05a9b367c64cf: Not on the island itself, but we do sometimes allow human vessels to dock here for trade. However, truth be told, I don't need to do much trading with them these days.
textByHash.57eb4562201eadb00b81e7f9c7607a0162a96c27: You must be the human that Korel spoke of. Welcome to the Conch.
textByHash.77e8ad8964b1a80a36f40de9c8c802e73131899c: Sorry, but I'm too busy to talk right now. I'm still getting things sorted after that gryphon attack.
textByHash.a38743004c560688d080dd2709779145a3464301: That is very generous of you, human. Thank you.
textByHash.c648e1c8c565e55f0740e774c2b4415b6d368d3e: Anyway, did you want to buy anything?
textByHash.caf99bbce6271f9be506d9215737d26d02982203: Thank you for helping with those repairs, human. It shouldn't be long now until I can start selling my goods again.
textByHash.e8f76fe4e3a003ad643a91c9fae4659a276663af: It's a lot of effort, and at my age, there is little need in trying to make a large profit. As long as I have enough to get by, I'm happy.
textByHash.ff7623baf9ad42235fa75da6236fd4d43325abfd: Hello there, human. Interested in some fish? Or maybe you need some fishing supplies?
